response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with genuine strengths in testing culture, mentoring, and some high-throughput systems work, but he misses the role's core minimum qualifications by a wide margin: his professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, far short of the required 8 years, and his infrastructure exposure (low-latency DLang services, Kubernetes deployments of his own platform) is adjacent to but not the sustained large-scale cloud compute platform experience the role demands. There is also no formal technical-leadership role leading cloud or platform engineers, and an unexplained current career gap.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and a track record of mentoring through workshops and courses
Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of requests per second
Breadth across stacks and long-running ownership of an open-source geo-data platform deployed on Kubernetes
concerns: C++ experience is roughly 7 months professionally, versus the 8-year minimum requirement
No formal technical leadership role for a cloud/compute platform team; leadership evidence is limited to mentoring and workshops
Career trajectory centers on web/full-stack product work in TypeScript/Node/DLang rather than cloud infrastructure, plus an unexplained ongoing career gap since October 2025

key_factors.factor: Limited cloud compute platform and formal tech-lead experience
Solid distributed/low-latency systems and mentoring background
